threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still stare at the Tube map like it’s a treasure map, not realizing none of the lines go there anymore.
Caught a night bus last week, and a guy was trying to impress a girl with a packet of Skittles, claiming they were 'retro candy'. Awkward, but she seemed into it.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Sitting at the Tempelhofer Feld, I watched locals fly kites while an old man argued passionately with a pigeon. That's when I felt truly at home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop that doubles as a gallery, showcasing local artists while you munch on that juicy döner.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they can navigate BART delays without breaking a sweat; the latest chaos is just another Tuesday for us.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus stuck behind a street performer who was juggling flaming torches. Only in SF do delays turn into a free show downtown.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often miss that Gràcia's fountains are actually for filling up your bottle, not just for looks. They waste time buying water!

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their bewilderment at the late-night tapa prices—still expecting cheap menus like in other cities.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot a newbie by their confusion at the bus stops—standing there, looking for a Tube map that doesn’t exist.
Caught the 35 last night, and a bloke serenaded us all with his guitar—only in Brixton do you get live music at 2 AM on a bus.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I heard a toddler confidently yell 'Scheiß BVG!' at their mom while the tram was delayed, I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see people casually setting up a raves in parks, complete with sound systems, regardless of local laws. It’s a wild mix of chaos and community.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they know to avoid BART during morning rush if there's a slightest whiff of smoke. It's a survival skill.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Spotted a guy in a full pirate outfit running for the 49, parrot on his shoulder. Only in the Tenderloin does the bus feel like an adventure.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the fountains in Gràcia are just for looking. Locals use them to cool off, especially when the city gets too hot and packed.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still think it's acceptable to order a café con leche after 11 AM—classic rookie mistake in Gràcia.
